title: Ord Antalaha

|affiliation=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] (Formerly)<ref name="Republic">While ''Pirates & Privateers'' does not specify which entity colonized Ord Antalaha as an [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' indicate that these outposts were established by the Galactic Republic.</ref>
}}
{{Quote|A pleasant world with a light and scattered population, this world has one of the highest criminal-to-citizen ratios in the galaxy. This world may offer Alliance personnel a temporary refuge or supply exchange base. Caution: The criminal element is very protective toward the colonists and hostile to any perceived threat against them.|From a Rebel Alliance SecuriDex traveling advisory on Ord Antalaha|<ref name="PP " />}}
'''Ord Antalaha''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located along the [[Shwuy Exchange/Legends|Shwuy Exchange]], in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], that was colonized and established as an [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. As a military outpost, Ord Antalaha was primarily used for storage and recreational purposes, prompting the construction of thousands of warehouses across the planet. After the Clone Wars, all military forces abandoned the planet, leaving the civilian colonists as the planet's only inhabitants. The colonist population, however, quickly declined over the years and Ord Antalaha soon became a regular stopover and haven for [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], and other [[Realspace/Legends|space]]-faring criminals.

This new wave of [[The fringe|fringe]] elements soon inhabited Ord Antalaha's unoccupied warehouses, arming and barricading them into impenetrable [[Fortress/Legends|fortresses]] referred to as "[[Resort]]s." The colonists, despite the arrival of their new neighbors, continued living their lives in peace as simple [[Farm/Legends|farmers]]. In fact, the majority of Ord Antalaha's criminal population found it wise to prevent the harassment of the colonists for fear of attracting unwanted [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] attention. Though no formal government ruled over the planet after the Clone Wars, the collective fringe presence on the world operated under a code consisting of three fundamental rules. Ord Antalaha's sole [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]] was [[Fort Divin]], a center of [[Black market/Legends|black marketeering]] and smuggling.

==Description==
Ord Antalaha was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Ord Antalaha system/Legends|Ord Antalaha system]]<ref name="Atlas 236" /> located along the [[Shwuy Exchange/Legends|Shwuy Exchange]], a minor [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade route]]<ref name="PP " /> in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] region<ref name="Atlas 236" /> connecting the [[Shwuy sector/Legends|Shwuy]] and [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir]] [[Sector/Legends|sectors]]. While Ord Antalaha was remote, the fact that it accessed a minor trade route meant that [[Starship/Legends|starships]] could use it as a stopover on their way to the two major routes toward either end of the Exchange:<ref name="PP " /> the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] and the [[Namadii Corridor/Legends|Namadii Corridor]].<ref name="Atlas ">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

A pleasant world with a temperate climate and a breathable [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], Ord Antalaha was covered in a mix of forests and plains spread out across its three continents.<ref name="PP " /> Originally colonized by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="Republic" /> as an [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]], the planet later became a haven for [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], and other [[The fringe|fringe]] elements. After its abandonment by the Republic, Ord Antalaha was ruled by no individual government, although local fiefdoms arose across the planet.<ref name="PP " /> The planet's major industries included [[Farm/Legends|farming]] and illegal ship services, and planetary trade mainly dealt in stolen goods and plunder.<ref name="PP " /> The only [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]] located on the planet was [[Fort Divin]].<ref name="PP " />

The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[SecuriDex]] directory identified Ord Antalaha as only a moderate threat to Alliance personnel and considered the world to be strategically located. It was also recommended as a temporary refuge or the site of a supply exchange base for Alliance [[operative]]s.<ref name="PP " />

==History==
Ord Antalaha was colonized by the Galactic Republic as an Ordnance/Regional Depot sometime before the [[Pius Dea Era]],<ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> and was initially used as a storage facility and recreational zone for wounded [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] due to its convenient location along the Shwuy Exchange. All over the planet, thousands of warehouses were constructed and clustered by the dozen. These warehouses were eventually evenly relocated across the planet and lightly [[Armor/Legends|armored]] to slow potential [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] by enemy forces. A small [[Medcenter/Legends|hospital]] and resort was also constructed for the benefit recovering soldiers. Over the course of the [[List of battles/Legends|war]], Ord Antalaha remained nearly undisturbed; it was the site of several staging actions but no actual battles took place there. After the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the military bases and warehouses were downgraded and eventually abandoned, but the colony remained and subsisted on light trade and agriculture.<ref name="PP " />

[[File:OrdAntalahaFarms.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Much of Ord Antalaha's colonist population continued living in peace as farmers after the end of the Clone Wars.]]
The colony's population began to rapidly decline in the years following the Clone Wars until there was virtually no colony left,<ref name="PP " /> and the few remaining colonists moved elsewhere around the planet.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|419|Ord Antalaha}}</ref> Ord Antalaha eluded the attention of the newly-established [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], but soon became a regular stop for pirates and smugglers. The world's small existing population allowed for criminals to conduct their private business without local or Imperial interference, and as word spread among the underworld, the abandoned assortment of defensible warehouses and staging facilities were repaired and reinhabited, serving as bases for various pirate and smuggling groups. The colonists continued living as they did before the arrival of fringe elements, and were left undisturbed by their new neighbors.<ref name="PP " />

==Inhabitants==
After the Clone Wars, Ord Antalaha was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to a light, scattered population of colonists and criminals, both [[Human/Legends|Human]] and [[Alien/Legends|otherwise]].<ref name="PP " /> The planet was known to have one of the highest criminal-to-citizen ratios in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="PP " /> Between approximately [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="Rebellion">These are the establishment and reorganization dates of the Rebel Alliance, respectively given by the [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game]] and ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. This section of ''Pirates & Privateers'' gives statistics on Ord Antalaha as it was during the existence of the Rebellion.</ref> Ord Antalaha's inhabitants were thought to have numbered around 150,000, although the accuracy of this number was in question.<ref name="PP " /> By circa [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the average inhabited world on the Shwuy Exchange had a population between 100 million and 500 million.<ref name="Atlas " />

===Colonists===
Most of Ord Antalaha's colonist population was based five [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] away from the Fort Divin starport and located within the planet's warm forests and hot plains. After all military forces abandoned the planet in the aftermath of the Clone Wars,<ref name="PP " /> the colonists left their original dwellings<ref name="CSWE" /> but continued living their lives in relative peace, although their numbers rapidly dwindled over the years. Many colonists lived as simple farmers<ref name="PP " /> and sustained themselves through agriculture in the absence of Republic support. Virtually nothing changed in their lifestyle after the arrival of pirates and other criminals; aside from the occasional low-flying pirate ship, their territory remained peaceful and quiet. Although they did not particularly enjoy living near such rough individuals, the colonists found their criminal neighbors tolerable. Spread out across the planet were roughly two thousand occupied warehouses, collectives, farms, homesteads, ranches, and villages, each with colonist populations ranging from one to one thousand. Wherever possible, the colonists attempted to move into the abandoned Republic facilities, but many simply used prefabricated buildings when warehouses were not available.<ref name="PP " />

===Criminals===
Criminal elements, such as pirates and smugglers, were originally drawn to Ord Antalaha due to the planet's relative obscurity and convenient location along the Shwuy Exchange. Since the planet had a very tiny colonist population and was entirely overlooked by the Empire, criminals could go about their business without conflict.<ref name="PP " />

Though no actual government existed after the Republic abandonment, a local code was developed among the criminals to govern the various fringe elements on the planet. The code consisted of three basic rules, the first one being "''Antalaha is a neutral zone''." Feuds between criminal groups were prohibited on the planet; pirate [[Captain/Legends|captains]] and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] units often clashed in [[Realspace/Legends|space]], but any conflict between them was to be suspended upon reaching the surface of Ord Antalaha. Personal duels and [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] brawls were permitted, but any organized aggression was met with harsh punishment by the rest of the planet's criminal denizens. The second rule was "''Leave the colonists alone''." Many felt that allowing the colonists to live in peace would keep unwanted attention away from their safe haven. Most were concerned that outraged farmers could attract Imperial interest, which they felt would end in disaster for everyone. Some pirates and mercenaries took this idea to an extreme and believed that it was their duty to protect the colonists from [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]] and [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]]. Though the colonists often felt overprotected, most of them preferred having the criminals occupy the planet rather than the Imperials. The final rule enforced by the fringe elements of Ord Antalaha was "''You only get one chance''." Overly disruptive individuals were given one warning by the planet's older criminal denizens; no second failures were tolerated and repeat offenders were sometimes known to go missing.<ref name="PP " />

[[File:FortDivin.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Criminals at Fort Divin, Ord Antalaha's only starport]]
Many criminals made their homes in the warehouses abandoned by the Republic. About one thousand warehouse clusters, consisting of one to ten warehouses each, were spread across the planet, each varying in size and design. Roughly five hundred of these were occupied by the various raider, [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]], smuggler, and mercenary groups drawn to the planet. These inhabited clusters were often referred to as "[[Resort]]s" by the colonists. Each Resort had at least one large warehouse, usually subdivided into quarters and run by a support staff, and a landing field large enough for a [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]]. Additionally, many Resorts had at least a single [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] and some criminal groups even installed light [[space defense blaster]]s in case of attack. A few group leaders were paranoid enough to move periodically; others set up their Resorts in such a fashion that evacuation could be achieved at a moment's notice. Not all groups transformed their Resorts into impenetrable [[Fortress/Legends|fortresses]]: others turned their claimed structures into simple settlements or miniature courts.<ref name="PP " />

==Locations==
===Fort Divin===
{{Main|Fort Divin}}
Ord Antalaha's sole starport, Fort Divin was named after [[Divin]], the first [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] to command the planet's military depot after its initial colonization. After the arrival of off-world criminals, the starport began to receive a good deal of illegal traffic. Smuggling eventually became a secondary industry on the planet and a large number of [[black marketeer]]s began to trade with the local pirates for their plunder. Fort Divin was the site of a fair amount of illicit dealing and backstabbing, but it was not considered much more dangerous than most seedy starports. [[Transponder code/Legends|Transponder]] forgeries and ship modifications were among the starport's many illegal offerings, but the port's facilities were unable to service any ships larger than a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] and mainly dealt with converted [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] and [[Patrol ship/Legends|patrol vessels]]. Fort Divin had a thriving used starship market, although ships were sold stripped so that weapons and other extras had to be added on later, at a small profit to the starship fitters.<ref name="PP " />

==Behind the scenes==
Ord Antalaha was first detailed in ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'' by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]], a supplement to [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]] published by [[West End Games]] in [[1997]]. In [[2008]] it received an entry in the multi-author work ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' and in [[2009]] it was referenced in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]].

''Pirates & Privateers'' states that fringe elements did not discover Ord Antalaha until "roughly ten years before [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] ascension to the throne." However, since the rest of the book indicates that the criminals only occupied the planet when it was abandoned after the Clone Wars, this is likely a mistake.<ref name="PP " /> This is further reinforced by the planet's entry in ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'', which indicates that the planet was discovered by pirates after the Clone Wars.<ref name="CSWE" />
